title: PCI DSS v4.0.1 Master Assessment (Complete 12 Requirements)

sections.title: Requirement 1: Install and Maintain Network Security Controls

items.text: Security policies and operational procedures for Requirement 1 are documented, kept up to date, in use, and known to all affected parties.
Roles and responsibilities for Requirement 1 are documented, assigned, and understood.
Configuration standards for NSC rulesets are defined, implemented, and maintained.
Changes to network connections and NSC configurations are managed via change control.
Accurate network diagrams showing connections between the CDE and other networks are maintained.
Accurate data-flow diagrams showing account data flows are maintained and updated.
All allowed services, protocols, and ports are identified, approved, and have a defined business need.
Security features are implemented for insecure protocols such that risk is mitigated.
NSC configurations are reviewed at least once every six months.
NSC configuration files are secured from unauthorized access and kept consistent with active configurations.
Inbound traffic to the CDE is restricted to necessary traffic; all other traffic is denied.
Outbound traffic from the CDE is restricted to necessary traffic; all other traffic is denied.
NSCs are installed between all wireless networks and the CDE.
NSCs are implemented between trusted and untrusted networks.
Inbound traffic from untrusted networks is restricted to authorized components or stateful responses.
Anti-spoofing measures are implemented to block forged source IP addresses.
System components storing cardholder data are not directly accessible from untrusted networks.
Internal IP address and routing information disclosure is limited to authorized parties.
Security controls are implemented on devices connecting to both untrusted networks and the CDE.

sections.title: Requirement 2: Apply Secure Configurations to All System Components

items.text: Security policies and procedures for Requirement 2 are documented and known.
Roles and responsibilities for Requirement 2 are documented and assigned.
Configuration standards are developed and maintained based on industry hardening standards.
Vendor default accounts are managed (changed if used, removed/disabled if not).
Primary functions with differing security levels are isolated or secured to the highest level.
Only necessary services and functions are enabled; unnecessary ones are disabled.
Insecure services have documented business justifications and mitigation controls.
System security parameters are configured to prevent misuse.
All non-console administrative access is encrypted using strong cryptography.
Wireless vendor defaults are changed at installation.
Wireless encryption keys are changed upon personnel changes or suspected compromise.
Requirement 3 policies and procedures are documented and known.
Data storage is minimized through retention and disposal policies; old data is verified as deleted quarterly.
Sensitive Authentication Data (SAD) is not stored after authorization, even if encrypted.
SAD stored electronically prior to authorization is encrypted.
PAN is masked when displayed; maximum display is BIN and last four digits.
Remote access technical controls prevent unauthorized copy/relocation of PAN.
PAN is rendered unreadable wherever stored via hashing, truncation, tokens, or strong cryptography.
Hashes used for PAN are keyed cryptographic hashes.
Cryptographic keys are protected against disclosure and misuse.
Secret and private keys are encrypted, stored in SCDs, or kept as key shares.
Key management procedures include generation, distribution, and secure storage of strong keys.
Keys are changed when cryptoperiods are reached or integrity is compromised.
Manual cleartext key management uses split knowledge and dual control.

sections.title: Requirement 4: Protect Cardholder Data with Strong Cryptography During Transmission

items.text: Requirement 4 policies and procedures are documented and known.
Strong cryptography and secure protocols are used for PAN transmission; invalid/expired certificates are blocked.
An inventory of trusted keys and certificates used for PAN transmission is maintained.
PAN is encrypted whenever sent via end-user messaging technologies.

sections.title: Requirement 5: Protect All Systems and Networks from Malicious Software

items.text: Requirement 5 policies and procedures are documented and known.
Anti-malware is deployed on all system components unless documented as unaffected.
Periodic evaluations ensure documented 'unaffected' components remain safe.
Anti-malware signatures and detection rules are kept up to date.
Anti-malware is actively running and cannot be disabled by users without authorization.
Anti-malware automatically alerts personnel when malware is detected.
Automated mechanisms detect and protect users against phishing attacks.

sections.title: Requirement 6: Develop and Maintain Secure Systems and Software

items.text: Requirement 6 policies and procedures are documented and known.
Software developers are trained annually in secure coding and job-specific security.
Custom code is reviewed prior to production release by independent parties or automated tools.
Techniques are used to prevent common attacks like injection, XSS, and CSRF.
Vulnerabilities are identified and managed via risk ranking.
Inventory of custom and third-party software components is maintained.
Critical security patches are installed within one month; others are installed based on risk.
Automated technical solutions (e.g., WAF) protect public-facing web applications.
Payment page scripts are authorized, integrity-checked, and inventoried.
Production changes follow established procedures including security impact analysis.
Pre-production and production environments are strictly separated.
Live PANs are not used in pre-production environments.

sections.title: Requirement 7: Restrict Access to System Components and Cardholder Data

items.text: Requirement 7 policies and procedures are documented and known.
Access is granted based on job function and least privilege.
User accounts and access privileges are reviewed at least every six months.
Application and system accounts are managed via least privilege.
Access to CHD repositories is restricted to responsible administrators.
Access control systems are set to 'deny all' by default.

sections.title: Requirement 8: Identify Users and Authenticate Access

items.text: Requirement 8 policies and procedures are documented and known.
Users are assigned unique IDs before access is allowed.
Access for terminated users is immediately revoked.
Inactive accounts are disabled/removed within 90 days.
Idle sessions exceeding 15 minutes require re-authentication.
Lockout occurs after max 10 failed attempts; duration is min 30 minutes.
Passwords meet complexity: min 12 characters, numeric and alphabetic.
New passwords cannot match the last four used.
Single-factor passwords change every 90 days or use dynamic posture analysis.
MFA is implemented for all non-console access to the CDE.
MFA is implemented for all remote access that could impact the CDE.
MFA systems are configured to prevent replay and bypass.
Passwords for interactive system accounts are not hard coded.

sections.title: Requirement 9: Restrict Physical Access to Cardholder Data

items.text: Requirement 9 policies and procedures are documented and known.
Facility entry/exit is monitored via video/physical controls; logs kept for 3 months.
Visitor logs record activity and are retained for three months.
Security of offline media backup locations is reviewed annually.
Inventories of electronic media with CHD are conducted annually.
Hard-copy materials with CHD are destroyed (e.g., cross-cut shredded) when no longer needed.
POI devices are protected from tampering via lists, inspections, and training.

sections.title: Requirement 10: Log and Monitor All Access to System Components

items.text: Requirement 10 policies and procedures are documented and known.
Audit logs are active for all system components and CHD.
Logs capture all administrative actions and interactive use of system accounts.
Logs are promptly backed up to secure, central log servers.
Automated mechanisms perform daily audit log reviews.
Logs are retained for 12 months; last 3 months are immediately available.
System clocks are synchronized via time-synchronization technology.
Failures of critical security controls are detected and alerted promptly.

sections.title: Requirement 11: Test Security of Systems and Networks Regularly

items.text: Requirement 11 policies and procedures are documented and known.
Wireless access points are identified and monitored quarterly.
Internal vulnerability scans are performed via authenticated scanning.
External vulnerability scans are performed quarterly by an ASV.
Penetration testing methodology is defined and implemented.
Internal and external penetration tests are performed annually.
Segmentation controls are penetration tested annually.
Change-detection (FIM) alerts on unauthorized modification of critical files.
Tamper-detection alerts on unauthorized changes to payment pages.

sections.title: Requirement 12: Support Information Security with Organizational Policies

items.text: An overall information security policy is established and disseminated.
Targeted risk analysis is performed annually for flexible frequency requirements.
Cipher suites and protocols are reviewed annually against industry trends.
PCI DSS scope is documented and confirmed annually.
Security awareness training is provided upon hire and annually.
A list of all third-party service providers (TPSPs) is maintained.
TPSP compliance status is monitored annually.
An incident response plan is established and ready for activation.
Incident response plan is tested at least annually.
Procedures are in place to respond if PAN is found where not expected.
